zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Bell於1490年出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾瑟斯克時，他的父親Sir Lord William F. Bell30歲，母親Frances Jannett Coney20歲。他和Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson至少有6個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1584年2月3日在英國諾福爾可諾維其諾維其 卡素享年94歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德斯坦寧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Bell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and Scottish (Lowlands): from the Middle English personal name Bell. As a man's name this is from Old French beu, bel ‘handsome’, which was also used as a nickname. As a female name it represents a short form of Isabel .

English (northern) and Scottish (Lowlands): from Middle English belle ‘bell’ (Old English belle), in various applications; most probably a metonymic occupational name for a bell ringer or bell maker, or a topographic name for someone living ‘at the bell’ (as attested by 14th-century forms such as John atte Belle). This indicates either residence by an actual bell (e.g. a town's bell in a bell tower, centrally placed to summon meetings, sound the alarm, etc.) or ‘at the sign of the bell’, i.e. a house or inn sign (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in Scots and English).
English: from Middle English bel ‘fair, fine, good’ (Old French bel ‘beautiful, fair’). See also Beal 1.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfonso, Alvarado, Augusto, Belia, Josefina, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Romero, Santa.

French, Spanish, Portuguese, and English (of French origin): from the Old French female personal name Isabel, which is first recorded in late 11th-century Paris and Maine (France). It was an alternative form of Elizabeth , Old French Elisabet, which had a shortened pet form Isabet. Isabel appears to have originated as a variant of Isabet, in which the suffix -el was substituted for -et through re-interpretation of -et as a hypocoristic diminutive. The resultant -bel was early associated with Old French bel ‘fair, beautiful’, as is evident in the French variant Isabeau. From France the name was taken to England, where it achieved considerable popularity in the late medieval period as an independent personal name alongside its rarer doublet Elizabeth. In the form Isabel it also appears in Spain and Portugal, while in Italy it took the form Isabella . The French surnames Isabel and Isabelle are particularly associated with Normandy.
History: Michel Isabel from Reux in Calvados, France, married Marie Jobidon in Château-Richer, QC, in 1673.

variants.name: Beller
meanings: English: occupational name for a bell founder or bell ringer, from Middle English beller, an agent derivative of Old English belle ‘bell’. Compare Bell 2.
German: habitational name for someone from any of several places called Bell in North Rhine-Westphalia or Bellen near Bremen.
German: nickname for a quarreller, from Middle High German bellen ‘to bicker, quarrel’.

variants.name: Bells
meanings: English: variant of Bell with genitival or excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bellman
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a bell ringer, in particular one whose duty was to make public announcements, after ringing a bell to attract attention (from Middle English bel(le)man, a term known to have been used in York and Scotland, and probably elsewhere, for a town crier). Compare Bell .
English: occupational name from Middle English beli-man ‘bellows blower’, from Middle English beli ‘bellows’. Compare Bellow .
Altered form of German   Bellmann  . This surname is also found in Sweden.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Bell was born in 1490, in Thirsk, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Lord William F. Bell, was 30 and his mother, Frances Jannett Coney, was 20. He had at least 6 sons and 2 daughters with Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson. He died on 3 February 1584, in Norwich Castle, Norwich, Norfolk, England, at the age of 94, and was buried in Stannington, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé le 3 février 1584, à Norwich Castle, Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 94 ans, et a été enterré à Stannington, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bellが1490年にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアスイルスクで生まれた時，父Sir Lord William F. Bellは30歳，母Frances Jannett Coneyは20歳であった。彼はElizabeth Cromwell Andersonとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1584年2月3日にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチノーウィチ カストルで94歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドスタニントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bell(이)가 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 스이르스크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Lord William F. Bell(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Frances Jannett Coney(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1584년 2월 3일 영국 노르폴크 노위치 노위치 카스틀에서 94세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 스탠잉턴에 묻혔다.

Bell было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Frances Jannett Coney, 20. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери от Elizabeth Cromwell Anderson.
